bead.title: default-tenant-bootstrap: concurrency-safe zero-tenant auto-bootstrap
bead.description:
Concurrency-safe auto-bootstrap for zero-tenant deployments. On first authenticated request, create a default tenant + default database + add the caller as admin. All under ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING so concurrent first-requests converge on a single row.

## Scope

### 1. New module: crates/axon-server/src/bootstrap.rs

    //! Default-tenant auto-bootstrap per ADR-018 §6.
    //!
    //! On a fresh deployment with zero tenants, the first authenticated
    //! request creates:
    //!   1. A "default" tenant row in the auth_schema tenants table
    //!   2. A "default" database in tenant_databases
    //!   3. A tenant_users row adding the caller as admin
    //!
    //! Idempotent — runs only when storage.count_tenants() returns 0, and
    //! concurrency-safe via ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING.

    pub async fn ensure_default_tenant(
        storage: &dyn StorageAdapter,
        caller_user_id: UserId,
    ) -> Result<DefaultTenantHandle, AxonError>;

    pub struct DefaultTenantHandle {
        pub tenant_id: TenantId,
        pub database_name: String,
    }

The caller identity comes from B1 (ResolvedIdentity) for JWT flow or B3 (resolve_tailscale_identity) for Tailscale. In either case, by the time we reach bootstrap, the user row exists.

### 2. Storage adapter: add a fast count + upsert path

    fn count_tenants(&self) -> Result<usize, AxonError> {
        Ok(0)
    }

    fn upsert_default_tenant(
        &self,
        name: &str,  // always "default"
    ) -> Result<TenantId, AxonError>;

upsert_default_tenant SQL:

    INSERT INTO tenants (id, name, display_name, created_at_ms, updated_at_ms)
    VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
    ON CONFLICT (name) DO NOTHING;

    SELECT id FROM tenants WHERE name = ?;

The first call wins with its generated UUID; subsequent callers get the winning UUID back. Wrap in a transaction.

### 3. ensure_default_tenant logic

    1. count_tenants() — if > 0, we're NOT in bootstrap; return Err(AxonError::NotFound) so caller uses the explicit tenant URL instead.
    2. upsert_default_tenant("default") — get the tenant_id.
    3. create_tenant_database(tenant_id, "default") — ignore ON CONFLICT (idempotent).
    4. upsert_tenant_member(tenant_id, caller_user_id, TenantRole::Admin) — idempotent.
    5. Return DefaultTenantHandle { tenant_id, database_name: "default" }.

Each step uses an idempotent write, and all callers converge on the same (tenant_id, database_name) pair.

### 4. Tests: crates/axon-server/tests/bootstrap_test.rs (NEW)

#### bootstrap_first_call_creates_everything
Empty DB → ensure_default_tenant → verify tenants has 1 row, tenant_databases has 1 row, tenant_users has 1 row.

#### bootstrap_second_call_returns_same_handle
Call twice with different user IDs → first call creates; second call adds the second user as admin without creating a new tenant. Verify tenants still has 1 row, tenant_users now has 2 rows.

#### bootstrap_skips_when_tenants_present
Seed with an explicit tenant → ensure_default_tenant returns NotFound error (not the default tenant). The middleware caller will then use the explicit URL path.

#### concurrent_bootstrap_converges (INV-020 primitive)
Spawn N=64 threads calling ensure_default_tenant with 64 distinct user UUIDs on a fresh DB. After join:
- Exactly 1 tenant row with name="default"
- Exactly 1 tenant_databases row
- 64 tenant_users rows (one per caller), all pointing at the same tenant_id
- Every caller returned the same tenant_id in their DefaultTenantHandle

Run on memory AND sqlite backends. This is INV-020 at the primitive level; the full axon-sim workload is a separate bead.

#### bootstrap_uses_default_database_name
Assert DefaultTenantHandle::database_name == "default".

## Out of scope

- Router wiring: this bead is the primitive + tests. The HTTP middleware/router that DECIDES when to call ensure_default_tenant (based on path prefix or config) is D3's job after the path-router is finalized.
- --no-auth integration: E2 handles the no-auth path-based namespace. E1 is the auth-mode bootstrap.
- Deleting the "default" tenant: out of scope. The default tenant behaves like any other tenant for deletion purposes (uses the legacy path).

## Context

- A1 schema: tenants table has UNIQUE(name) — that's the conflict target.
- A2 types: TenantId, TenantRole, User, etc.
- B1: ResolvedIdentity has user_id
- B3: upsert_user_identity creates the user row before bootstrap runs
- B4: upsert_tenant_member is what we call for the admin grant
- C1: list_tenant_members exists (for tests)
- C2: create_tenant_database exists (call it here)

## Files to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/bootstrap.rs — NEW
- crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs — pub mod bootstrap; re-export
- crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs — count_tenants + upsert_default_tenant trait methods
- crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs — impl
- crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs — impl
- crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs — impl
- crates/axon-server/tests/bootstrap_test.rs — NEW

## Files NOT to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — router wiring is D3
- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical
- crates/axon-server/src/control_plane.rs — legacy
- crates/axon-server/src/control_plane_routes.rs — tenant CRUD handlers are C1/C2 territory

## Verification

- cargo test -p axon-server --test bootstrap_test  (5 tests must pass)
- cargo test -p axon-storage  (2 new trait methods)
- cargo test --workspace  (all prior tests green — additive)
- cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean
bead.acceptance:
- [ ] bootstrap.rs with ensure_default_tenant + DefaultTenantHandle
- [ ] StorageAdapter: count_tenants + upsert_default_tenant
- [ ] All 3 backends implement
- [ ] bootstrap_test.rs with 5 tests (first call, second call, skips when tenants present, concurrent N=64, default name)
- [ ] Concurrent N=64 test runs on memory AND sqlite
- [ ] Uses ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING (no check-then-insert)
- [ ] cargo test --workspace green
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean
